featurePanels.title: Round Read
items: Round 13 was messy but profitable: the tip card went 5 from 7 and the Penrith charity bet landed 20-18
Round 14 is a fuller eight-game slate with the Warriors on the bye
The Panthers are the cleanest raw tip with Nathan Cleary, Isaah Yeo and Brian To'o all named to return
Sharks and Broncos are strong leans, but both are coming out of emotionally strange Round 13 results

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: Do not automatically roll the $10.53 TAB collect into another bet
Sea Eagles versus Rabbitohs is loaded with returning South Sydney personnel and Manly home-ground edge
Cowboys versus Dolphins is a genuine volatility game with major Dolphins ins and Townsville travel
Bulldogs versus Eels is a Monday trap because Parramatta were better than the 28-22 loss suggests

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Protect the newly positive charity ledger instead of forcing a Round 14 play
Wait for TAB prices and final 24-hour teams before any new stake
Track whether Brisbane respond mentally after the Dragons loss
Keep the Round 13 Penrith charity win settled and do not rewrite it during Round 14 preview work

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Penrith spine and leadership back against Wests Tigers
Dolphins backline and middle reinforcements against North Queensland
Storm home response with Nick Meaney out but Grant, Munster and Hughes intact
Dragons confidence after their first win of the season

betCard.title: Round 14 Charity Bet
betCard.kickoff: Round runs Thursday 4 June to Monday 8 June 2026
why: Round 13 produced the correct betting outcome: Penrith H2H won at $1.62 and returned $10.53.
The ledger is finally positive at +$0.53, so the correct default is to protect it.
Panthers H2H and Sharks H2H can be price-watched, but neither becomes a charity bet without a fresh TAB price and user confirmation.
risks: Do not roll the full $10.53 collect into a new bet by default.
Round 14 has several rivalry and response spots where favourites can be over-short.
Any new charity play needs recorded odds, stake, match, and final team context before kickoff.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
charity.nextDonation: After a meaningful positive bank is built
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.
Round 7: Wests Tigers head to head at $1.53 lost in a 21-20 defeat to Brisbane.
Round 8: No charity bet placed, bank held at $6.50.
Round 9: Bulldogs head to head at $1.77 lost after a Cowboys comeback win.
Rounds 10 and 11: No charity bet locked, so the ledger stayed unchanged.
Round 12: No charity bet locked; tips went 5 from 5, but the ledger stayed unchanged.
Round 13: Penrith Panthers head to head at $1.62 won 20-18, returning $10.53 from a $6.50 stake.
After Round 13: Ledger is 3-2, $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.

watchlist: Price-watch Panthers over Wests Tigers only if TAB offers enough value; it is likely to be very short.
Price-watch Sharks over Dragons, but respect the derby and the Dragons' first-win confidence bump.
Do not bet Sea Eagles versus Rabbitohs, Cowboys versus Dolphins, or Bulldogs versus Eels without a clear market edge.
Keep the $10.53 Round 13 collect separate from any new Round 14 decision.
Warriors are on the bye, so no Warriors tip or bet this week.
confidence: Panthers over Wests Tigers - strongest raw read with Cleary, Yeo and To'o named back
Sharks over Dragons - home derby lean, Dragons confidence caveat
Broncos over Titans - Suncorp response spot, Titans regain Tino and Jojo
Sea Eagles over Rabbitohs - home edge in a close Thursday opener
Roosters over Raiders - stronger spine, Canberra danger acknowledged
Storm over Knights - AAMI lean against a hot Newcastle side
Dolphins over Cowboys - reinforcements edge, Townsville risk
Bulldogs over Eels - lowest-confidence Monday tip

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 13 banked the charity bet; Round 14 starts from discipline
lines: As of Wednesday, 3 June 2026, Round 13 has been settled and Round 14 team lists have been checked.
TipBot went 5 from 7 in Round 13: Sharks, Knights, Storm, Raiders and Panthers were correct; Bulldogs and Broncos missed.
The locked Penrith charity bet won 20-18 at $1.62, returning $10.53 from a $6.50 stake.
Round 14 is live as a preview board only; no new charity bet is locked.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: The ledger is positive, so no forced follow-up
lines: The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger is now 3-2 with $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.
The cleanest raw Round 14 tip is Panthers over Wests Tigers, but the market is likely to be short.
Sharks over Dragons is the next price-watch candidate, but the Dragons finally won and derby variance is real.
No Round 14 charity bet should be recorded without a fresh TAB price, stake and user confirmation.

sections.title: Round 14 Fixtures
sections.heading: Official Round 14 fixture board
lines: Sea Eagles v Rabbitohs - Thursday 4 June, 7:50pm at 4 Pines Park.
Storm v Knights - Friday 5 June, 6:00pm at AAMI Park.
Raiders v Roosters - Friday 5 June, 8:00pm at GIO Stadium.
Cowboys v Dolphins - Saturday 6 June, 5:30pm at Queensland Country Bank Stadium.
Broncos v Titans - Saturday 6 June, 7:35pm at Suncorp Stadium.
Wests Tigers v Panthers - Sunday 7 June, 2:00pm at CommBank Stadium.
Sharks v Dragons - Sunday 7 June, 4:05pm at Ocean Protect Stadium.
Bulldogs v Eels - Monday 8 June, 4:05pm at Accor Stadium.
Warriors have the bye.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who gets upgraded or held back this week
lines: Upgrade: Panthers, Sharks home profile, Storm at AAMI, and Dolphins personnel returning from the bye.
Hold: Knights, because the form is real but Melbourne away is a harder test than Parramatta at home.
Downgrade: Bulldogs after another loss, Eels without a stable top-line spine, and Titans despite Tino returning.
Volatility bucket: Sea Eagles v Rabbitohs, Cowboys v Dolphins, and Bulldogs v Eels.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The Round 14 team-list details driving the card
lines: Manly still have no Tom Trbojevic, but Tolu Koula and Haumole Olakau'atu are named.
South Sydney name Cameron Murray, Campbell Graham, Brandon Smith and David Fifita, but Latrell Mitchell remains out.
Penrith name Nathan Cleary, Isaah Yeo and Brian To'o back after beating the Warriors without them.
The Dolphins regain major strike through Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow, Herbie Farnworth, Selwyn Cobbo, Thomas Flegler and Max Plath.
The Titans regain Tino Fa'asuamaleaui and Jojo Fifita, but Brisbane at Suncorp remains the better side.
